§ 01Engagement Confirmed

Dear {{trusteeName}},

Following the conflict check and your confirmation of availability, this memo formalizes the engagement of {{trusteeFirm}} as the independent ESOP trustee for the {{companyName}} employee ownership transition.

Engaged
Trustee Engagement Active
{{engagementDate}}
{{estimatedCloseDate}}
{{feeArrangement}}
§ 02Trustee Responsibilities

As the independent ESOP trustee, your responsibilities include:

A.FMV determination. Engage an independent, ASA-accredited appraiser to determine the fair market value of the company. Review and approve the appraisal report.
B.Fiduciary review. Evaluate whether the transaction is fair to the ESOP and its participants from a financial and fiduciary standpoint under ERISA.
C.Document review. Review and negotiate the Letter of Intent, Stock Purchase Agreement, ESOP Trust Agreement, and all ancillary transaction documents.
D.Independent judgment. Exercise independent fiduciary judgment throughout the transaction. Forhemit coordinates but does not direct the trustee's analysis or conclusions.
E.Closing execution. Execute the transaction documents and direct the ESOP's participation at closing.
F.Post-close oversight. Ongoing fiduciary oversight of the ESOP following the transaction, including repurchase obligation monitoring and annual compliance.
§ 03Forhemit's Role

To ensure clarity around independence:

✓Forhemit IS: Transaction coordinator, timeline manager, lender package preparer, post-close stewardship provider.
✗Forhemit IS NOT: Buyer, seller's agent, ERISA fiduciary, legal advisor, tax advisor. Forhemit does not direct the trustee's independent judgment.
SBA Independence: Forhemit holds no equity, no board seat, no voting rights, and no managerial control in the company or ESOP trust. All fees are paid by the operating company as ordinary business expenses.
§ 04What the Trustee Receives

Forhemit will provide the following materials to support the trustee's engagement:

01Financial package. 3 years of financials, tax returns, QofE-normalized TTM EBITDA, projections.
02Operational overview. COOP, management depth assessment, key person dependency analysis.
03Deal structure. Capital stack, sources and uses, debt service model, repurchase obligation analysis.
04Legal overview. Corporate structure, entity conversion status, board resolutions.
05Lender package. SBA 7(a) indication status, lender terms, intercreditor framework.
§ 05Timeline
Capital committed — Day 30
Valuation confirmed — Day 60
Operations cleared — Day 90
Closing — Day 120
Note: The 120-day timeline is a target, not a hard deadline. Gates may be extended by mutual agreement. The trustee should not feel pressured to rush the fiduciary review to meet a calendar date.
§ 06Next Steps
1.Review this memo. Confirm that the terms and scope are consistent with your understanding of the engagement.
2.Execute the formal trustee engagement letter. A separate engagement letter will follow from your firm for Forhemit's records.
3.Begin appraiser selection. Forhemit will provide a shortlist of ASA-accredited firms experienced in ESOP valuations. The trustee makes the final selection.
4.Receive the full deal package. Forhemit will deliver the complete financial, operational, and legal package within 5 business days of this memo.
